It covers the overall system design, deployment models, and technology choices that power the application. For detailed information about specific subsystems, see: Architecture details: Architecture Development setup and workflows: Getting Started Feature implementation: Core Features API reference: Backend API Frontend details: Frontend Application What is Voicebox? Voicebox is a local-first voice cloning and synthesis studio that enables users to clone voices and generate speech entirely on their machine. The system is designed as a privacy-focused alternative to cloud-based voice synthesis services, combining professional-grade audio editing capabilities with state-of-the-art machine learning models. Key Design Principles: Local-First : All ML inference, voice data, and generation history remain on the user's machine README.md 67 No Python Required : Distributed as standalone executables with an embedded Python runtime bundled via PyInstaller README.md 75 CHANGELOG.md 14-15 Platform-Optimized : Uses MLX on Apple Silicon and PyTorch on other platforms for optimal performance README.md 76 API-First : Full REST API enables integration with external tools and workflows README.md 74 Primary Use Cases: Voice cloning and text-to-speech generation using 5+ engines (Qwen3-TTS, LuxTTS, Chatterbox, etc.) README.md 65-69 Multi-voice narrative composition with a DAW-like timeline editor README.md 73 Speech-to-text transcription using Whisper models README.md 126-131 Real-time audio post-processing (reverb, pitch shift, compression) via Spotify's pedalboard README.md 120-123 Sources: README.md 1-135 CHANGELOG.md 50-55 System Architecture Voicebox follows a sidecar architecture where a Tauri desktop application manages a Python FastAPI backend as a subprocess. The frontend React application communicates with both the Tauri Rust layer (for native OS features) and the Python backend (for ML operations). High-Level System Flow Sources: tauri/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json 1-66 package.json 1-44 README.md 177-189 Deployment Models Voicebox supports three deployment configurations: Mode Description Components Desktop App (Primary) Standalone Tauri application with embedded Python backend tauri/ + app/ + bundled voicebox-server binary Web App Browser-based version connecting to remote backend web/ workspace deploying @voicebox/app Remote Server Headless FastAPI server for network-based inference backend/ running as standalone service on port 17493 Desktop Application Architecture The desktop application bundles all dependencies into a single installer. On startup, Tauri manages the Python server lifecycle, detecting orphaned processes and ensuring graceful shutdown tauri/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json 16 Sources: tauri/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json 1-66 package.json 11-25 CHANGELOG.md 14-22 Core Capabilities Voice Profile Management Voice profiles store reference audio samples used for voice cloning. Each profile contains metadata, optional avatars, and audio samples used as prompts for the TTS engines README.md 97-107 Recent updates allow deleting generations from a profile which also cleans up associated DB rows and orphaned files CHANGELOG.md 31-32 Speech Generation The generation pipeline supports unlimited length via auto-chunking at sentence boundaries with crossfading README.md 137-145 It also includes a cancellation mechanism to stop queued or running jobs CHANGELOG.md 25-27 Story Timeline Editor A multi-track timeline for conversations and narratives. It allows non-destructive editing and composition of multi-voice projects README.md 73 Audio Transcription Whisper-based speech-to-text with various model sizes. Supports converting speech back to text locally README.md 126-131 Sources: README.md 65-145 CHANGELOG.md 25-32 Technology Stack Component Technology Version Location Desktop Shell Tauri 2.0
